item.title: Analytical Modeling for Suction Cup Designs for Skin-Interfaced Wearable Devices
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 27, July 2026.
SignificanceMedical-grade wearable devices require mounting strategies that ensure stable skin contact for accurate, long-term measurements while allowing gentle removal to avoid skin damage. Suction-based mounting is an attractive adhesive-free approach, ...

item.title: Dual Salt Bridges Govern Proton Gating and Calcium Leak in BsYetJ Across Bilayers and Live Cells
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 27, July 2026.
SignificanceCells tightly regulate calcium because it controls processes ranging from signaling to survival. We show that the membrane proteinBsYetJ uses two distinct electrostatic gates to couple proton sensing to calcium leak: One gate controls channel ...

item.title: Deviance Detection via Competitive Inhibition Between Local Neocortical Ensembles
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026.
SignificancePrompt, adaptive behavior to unexpected events depends on fast and efficient neural processing of sensory information. Neurons and circuits within Neocortex are known to amplify sensory input in a history-dependent manner, enhancing the ...

item.title: Skin Capillary Endothelial Cells Form a Network of Spatiotemporally Conserved Ca2+ Activity
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026.
SignificanceCa2+signaling in mammalian endothelial cells (ECs) regulates blood flow, force-sensing, and vessel permeability. While past studies have investigated EC Ca2+signaling in 2D monolayers and the 3D mouse brain, how Ca2+signaling is organized ...

item.title: Horseshoe Bats Foraging in the Wild Adjust Sensing to Separate Prey Echoes from Background Clutter
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026.
SignificanceAnimals must constantly pick out vital signals from a flood of background noise, but how they do this in nature is poorly understood. Using tiny GPS-microphone tags on wild greater horseshoe bats, we found that these animals finely adjust the ...

item.title: SLC11A2 Withholds Divalent Metals from Salmonella in the Gut Epithelium
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026.
SignificanceThere is limited source of trace metals in the gut for which invading pathogens and the infected host must compete. UsingSalmonella entericaas a model enteric pathogen, along with fluorescent sensors of metal ion availability, we traced the ...

item.title: Mycoelectronics: Bioprinted Living Fungal Bioelectronics for Artificial Sensation
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026.
SignificanceBiohybrid systems hold great promise for building intelligent sensors for healthcare and the environment. However, current eukaryote-based biohybrids suffer from slow growth, strict culture requirements, and short lifespans, limiting their ...

item.title: Collapsible Scissored Surfaces
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 25, June 2026.
SignificanceThe two-bar linkage, or scissor mechanism, is an elementary machine which can close to a one-dimensional state in which both legs are parallel. We describe a class of geometric metamaterials based on lattices of scissor mechanisms which ...

item.title: State-Switching Navigation Strategies in Caenorhabditis Elegans Are Beneficial for Chemotaxis
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 25, June 2026.
SignificanceAnimals switch between behavioral strategies when navigating sensory environments, but how these decisions are made and whether they benefit navigation remains unclear. We show thatCaenorhabditis elegansnavigate via internal states that ...

item.title: Scalable Networks of Multimodal Haptic Arrays for Plantar Sensory Substitution
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 25, June 2026.
SignificanceThe sense of touch in our feet is essential for safe movement, and its impairment in conditions such as stroke and spinal cord injury contributes to diminished quality of life. Substituting missing foot sensation requires not only sensors for ...

item.title: Toward Quantum Sensing of Electron Beams Using Solid-State Spins
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 25, June 2026.
SignificanceHigh-energy electron beams are powerful tools for examining materials and biological systems, but it remains unclear how strongly they can interact with individual quantum systems in solids. Unlocking this interaction could enable electron-...

item.title: RNF126 Is a Peroxisomal Fate Switch Enabling Redifferentiation Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 24, June 2026.
SignificanceThe dedifferentiation of cancer cells is a major hallmark of malignancy and a challenge for therapy. Here, we reveal that hypoxia drives HCC dedifferentiation by actively eliminating peroxisomes, the organelles that help define liver cell ...

item.title: Alpha-Synuclein Fibrils Induce Budding of Mitochondrial-Derived Vesicles
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 24, June 2026.
α-synuclein (α-syn) aggregation is a hallmark of synucleinopathies, a class of neurodegenerative disorders such as Parkinson’s disease (PD). Several lines of evidence indicate the involvement of mitochondria in the disease pathology. Despite extensive ...

item.title: Kernel Embeddings and the Separation of Measure Phenomenon
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 23, June 2026.
SignificanceTwo-sample testing examines whether two probability distributions on some feature space differ based on random samples. It is fundamental in statistics and machine learning, especially when feature spaces are complex. Such settings are ...

item.title: The Diffusion of Large Language Models in Published Academic Articles
item.description:
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 22, June 2026.
SignificanceUnderstanding how academic knowledge is produced is essential for maintaining public trust in science. Large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT are rapidly transforming academic writing, yet little is known about who uses these tools or ...
